pr.title: ft: improve Crazy Hand init command dispatch
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: PR #2492 adjusted Crazy Hand init command dispatch in `ftCh_Init_80156AD8`: it removed a `btn_pressed` inline helper, introduced a local macro for `HSD_PadMasterStatus[3].button`, cached the L-button mask check, corrected A/Z command mappings, and replaced inline `0`/`2` float literals with declared `ftCh_Init_804DA05C` and `ftCh_Init_804DA060` externs. The PR reported improved checkdiff score but explicitly did not claim a byte match; it was closed unmerged after a comment that the work was already done elsewhere.

key_files.role: Updated `ftCh_Init_80156AD8` command-dispatch source shape, button-mask checks, A/Z mapping, and float constant uses.
evidence_refs: changed_files: ftCh_Init.c +35/-37, 1 hunk
raw_diff: `btn_pressed` removed; `#define buttons (HSD_PadMasterStatus[3].button)` added; `l_pressed` cache added
raw_diff: A/Z command branches swapped relative to previous source
pr_body: describes fixing human command dispatcher button mapping and caching L-button check

key_files.role: Declared two float symbols used by the revised command dispatcher.
evidence_refs: changed_files: ftCh_Init.h +3/-0, 1 hunk
raw_diff: adds `extern f32 ftCh_Init_804DA05C;` and `extern f32 ftCh_Init_804DA060;`
pr_body: describes naming zero/two float constants used by Vec3 setup and random range math

what_changed.text: Removed the local `btn_pressed(u32 mask)` inline helper and replaced repeated helper calls with direct checks against a `buttons` macro bound to `HSD_PadMasterStatus[3].button`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed `static inline u32 btn_pressed(u32 mask)`
raw_diff: added `#define buttons (HSD_PadMasterStatus[3].button)` and `#undef buttons`

what_changed.text: Cached `buttons & HSD_PAD_L` in `u32 l_pressed` and reused it across the L+DPad command branches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added `u32 l_pressed = buttons & HSD_PAD_L;`
pr_body: says this caches the L-button check in the same style as the matched Master Hand dispatcher

what_changed.text: Changed several dispatcher conditionals from helper calls to direct mask expressions such as `(buttons & HSD_PAD_R) && (buttons & HSD_PAD_DPADUP)`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: branch conditions throughout `ftCh_Init_80156AD8` changed from `btn_pressed(...)` to direct `buttons & ...` checks

what_changed.text: Corrected the documented human command dispatcher mapping by swapping affected A-button and Z-button DPad branches: BackAirplane branches moved to Z, while `ftCh_Init_801597F0`/`ftCh_Init_80159F40` branches moved to A.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `HSD_PAD_A` BackAirplane branches changed to `HSD_PAD_Z`
raw_diff: `HSD_PAD_Z` calls to `ftCh_Init_801597F0`/`ftCh_Init_80159F40` changed to `HSD_PAD_A`
pr_body: says it fixes the human command dispatcher button mapping

what_changed.text: Replaced repeated `pos.z = 0` assignments with `pos.z = ftCh_Init_804DA05C` and replaced random range multiplier `2` with `ftCh_Init_804DA060`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: multiple `pos.z = 0` lines changed to `pos.z = ftCh_Init_804DA05C`
raw_diff: `rand * 2` changed to `rand * ftCh_Init_804DA060`
ftCh_Init.h diff: added extern declarations for both symbols

smart_moves.text: The PR kept the score/match claim narrow: it reported improvement for `ftCh_Init_80156AD8` only and explicitly called out that the function was still not a byte match.
evidence_refs: pr_body: checkdiff command and 98.99317% result
pr_body: known remaining drift notes full pad-word register allocation and derived mask temp registers
bot_report: `ftCh_Init_80156AD8` improved from 98.86% to 98.99%

smart_moves.text: The L-button cache was localized to the dispatcher and described as following a matched sibling style rather than as a broad global rule.
evidence_refs: pr_body: says caching the L-button check is in the same style as the matched Master Hand dispatcher
raw_diff: only `ftCh_Init_80156AD8` hunk changed

decomp_lessons.lesson: For similar command-dispatch ladders, a tiny source-shape change such as caching one common button mask can improve matching while preserving readable direct mask tests.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `u32 l_pressed = buttons & HSD_PAD_L;` reused across L+DPad branches
pr_body: checkdiff result 98.99317%
bot_report: score improved from 98.86% to 98.99%

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a dispatcher has many near-identical branches, verifying the button-to-action mapping can be part of matching cleanup, not just code style cleanup.
evidence_refs: pr_body: says it fixes human command dispatcher button mapping
raw_diff: A/Z branch conditions changed for BackAirplane and related action branches

decomp_lessons.lesson: Report partial matching progress honestly: include exact tool output and known remaining register/allocation drift rather than implying closure.
evidence_refs: pr_body: checkdiff command and percentage
pr_body: `ftCh_Init_80156AD8` is still not a byte match
pr_body: remaining drift isolated to pad-word register allocation and derived mask temp registers

naming_conventions.name: l_pressed
naming_conventions.convention: Short boolean-like local naming for a cached button-mask expression.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added `u32 l_pressed = buttons & HSD_PAD_L;`

naming_conventions.name: buttons
naming_conventions.convention: Temporary macro alias for `HSD_PadMasterStatus[3].button`, scoped by immediate `#undef` after the function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `#define buttons (HSD_PadMasterStatus[3].button)` before `ftCh_Init_80156AD8`
raw_diff: `#undef buttons` after `ftCh_Init_80156AD8`

naming_conventions.name: ftCh_Init_804DA05C / ftCh_Init_804DA060
naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names retained for float constants when no semantic name is evidenced in the PR.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: uses `ftCh_Init_804DA05C` for zero-valued Vec3 z setup
raw_diff: uses `ftCh_Init_804DA060` for random range multiplier
ftCh_Init.h diff: extern declarations use address-style names

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace a small helper wrapper with direct access to the underlying pad word to influence register allocation and mask temps.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed `btn_pressed` helper
raw_diff: direct `buttons & HSD_PAD_*` expressions
pr_body: remaining drift is full pad-word register allocation and derived mask temp registers
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_limit: The PR still did not byte-match; the tactic improved score but left register-allocation drift.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Cache only the shared L-button subexpression while leaving other button branches as direct mask checks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `l_pressed` reused for L+DPad branches only
pr_body: says this mirrors matched Master Hand dispatcher style
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_limit: The matched Master Hand sibling is cited by the PR body but not included in the loaded diff; do not generalize without checking the sibling source.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use known float data symbols for zero and two in expression sites where data ownership is presumed by the PR.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inline `0` and `2` replaced with `ftCh_Init_804DA05C` and `ftCh_Init_804DA060`
ftCh_Init.h diff: extern declarations added
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_limit: Loaded evidence does not show symbol metadata or section ownership for the float constants; this should remain a PR-specific observation, not a general standard.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Cache shared button-mask checks narrowly in input dispatchers when objdiff supports it
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2492, caching `buttons & HSD_PAD_L` as `l_pressed` inside `ftCh_Init_80156AD8` and using direct pad-word mask checks improved reported checkdiff score for the Crazy Hand command dispatcher, but did not reach a byte match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `l_pressed` cache and direct `buttons &` checks
pr_body: checkdiff 98.99317%
text_corpus bot report: 98.86% to 98.99%
pr_body: still not a byte match

accepted_candidate_records.title: Partial decomp PRs should preserve non-match and drift details

pr_body: `python configure.py && ninja` -> OK

rejection_notes: Do not mark `ftCh_Init_80156AD8` as matched from this PR; the PR body explicitly says it is still not a byte match.
Do not infer merge or accepted-source status; metadata shows `merged_at` null and the PR was closed.
Do not promote local `#define buttons` macro use as a global style rule; it is a PR-specific matching tactic and may conflict with broader naming/alias caution without further evidence.
Do not treat the float extern replacements as globally preferred over literals without section ownership or current-source confirmation.
